In the Fragmenta there are 405 symptoms of belladonna, in the 1st Edit. 650, in the 2nd Edit.1422, and in this 3rd Edit.1440.] Belladona. Vertigo. [Sicelius, Observ., Dec. iv, Cas.4-Ziegler, Beob., Leipz., 1787, pp.21-38.-R.Buchave, in Samml. br. Abh. f. pr. Aerzte, xiv, iv.-Henning, in Hufel. Journ., xxi, i.-Eb. Gmelin, in Acta Nat. Cur., vi, App.]. Vertigo; objects seem to sway hither and thither. Whirling in the head, vertigo with nausea, as after rapid turning round in a circle, or as after the morning sleep following a nocturnal debauch. Whirling in the head, and at the same time a similar whirling in the pit of the stomach; after rising it became so bad when walking, that she could not distinguish anything, everything vanished from her sight. Vertigo as if all whirled round in a circle (aft. 1 h.). He goes round in a circle. [De St. Martin, Journal de M,d. xviii, Ao-t.] [f.-h1]. Stupid and whirling in the head; she feels better in the open air, worse in the room (aft. 1/4 h.). Attacks of vertigo, when at rest and when moving. A giddy feeling in the whole head, like vertigo, when sitting. Vertigo and trembling of the hands, so that she could not do anything withthem. [Baldinger, Neues Magazin f. Aerzte, i, I St., p.30.]. When walking he staggers, holds on to the walls, complains of anxiety and vertigo, and often talks nonsense like a drunken person. [Baldinger, 1.c.]. She rises from bed in the morning and staggers as if intoxicated, hither and thither. [Greding, in Ludwigii Adversar. med. pr., i, P. iv, p.670 (14).]. Giddy swaying. [Mardorf, Diss. de Maniacis Giessensibus, Giesae, 1691.-Lottinger, Med. Chirurg. Wahrnehm., Altenb., ii, p.326.-Tib. Lambergen, Lectio inaug. sist. eph. pers. carcin., Groning., 1754.] (f-h1]. Attacks of vertigo with obtuseness of senses for some minutes (aft. 12 h.). All day long confusion of senses; he knows not what he is doing. Obtuseness of senses. [f.-h1]. Cloudiness of the head, with swelling of the glands in the nape (aft. 6 h.). Intoxication. Immediately after a meal as if intoxicated. On drinking the smallest quantity of beer immediate intoxication. Muddled head and intoxication as if from drinking wine, with bloated red face. {Commercium liter. Nor., , 1731.]. His whole head is dazed for many days. Muddled state as in intoxication. [Hochstetter, Obs. Med., Fft. obs.7.-May, in Hannover. Mag., 1773, No.97.Sicelius, 1.c.-De Launay d'Hermont, in Hist. de l'Acad. des Sc., 1756.-Albrecht, in Commerc. lit. Nor., 1731.-Buc'hoz, in Vicat, Plantes v,n,n. de la Suisse, p.183.]. Muddled state of sinciput as if an oppressive fog went hither and thither, especially under the frontal bone. Muddling of the head as from much brandy and tobacco smoke. Muddling and confusion of the whole head, as from the disagreeable feeling of commencing intoxication. Confusion of the head; worse during movement. Disinclination for all intellectual work. Weakness of mind and body. Weakness of mind. [Wierus, de Praestig. Daemonum, ili, cap.17.] (f-h1]. Stupefaction. [Wagner, Miscell. Nat. Cur., Dec. ii, ann.10, obs.108 (II)- Buchave, -Wierus, l.c.]. Confusion of mind. [Sicelius, l.c.]. Confusion of mind, so that he knows not whether he is dreaming or awake. {Moibanus, in Schenck, vii, obs.164.]. Confusion of the senses; sleepy and yet awake, he thinks he is dreaming. [Moibanus, l.c.]. His senses deceive him. [Ackermann, in Struve, Triumph der H., iii, p.303.]. Exalted, deluded phantasy conjures up a number of beautiful pictures to her. He imagines he sees ghosts and insects of various kinds. [Moibanus, Lc.]. Her nose appears transparent to her. He imagines he sees things not present. [Wiedemann, in Hufel. Jour., xxii, L.]. It seems to her that a spot on the left side of her head is transparent and spotted brown. He thinks he is riding an ox. [G-Ch, in Hufel. Jour., xvii, I.]. He does not know his own relations. [ Wierus, l.c.]. Want of consciousness; he sat as if in a dream. Unconsciousness. He often lay without sense, without consciousness. Loss of consciousness and convulsions in the arm, at night. [Greding, Lce., p.672.]. Extreme stupefaction of the senses. [Ollenroth, in Hufel. Jour., vii, 4.]. Unconsciousness. [Hasenest, in Acta Nat. Cur., ili, obs.35. -Grimm, in Acta Nat. Cur., vol. ii, obs.60.-(aft. 2 h.). Rau, in Acta Nat. Cur., x, obs.24.- Eb.Gmelin, |.c.-Hochstetter, l.c.]. Unconsciousness with convulsions of the limbs. [Buchave, I.c.] [f£h1]. Complete unconsciousness; she knows nothing going on. [Henning, l.c.]. Complete loss of reason. [Sauter, in Huf. Jour. xi, I, p.125, (I).-Buchave, Le.]. Loss of reason, for some weeks. [Rau, l.c.]. Insensibility. [Vicat, Plantes veneneuses de la Suisse, p.181.]. Stupidity. [Wagner, l.c. (1).]. During the headache her thoughts leave her; she forgets what she thought of shortly before, and cannot recollect herself. Distraction of the mind; he easily makes mistakes in his work, and forgets things that he has just undertaken to do. He thinks now of one thing now of another; he could not think of anything properly, and he immediately forgot all he had just thought of or read. Impaired memory. Very weak memory; he forgets what he intended to do immediately, an can remember nothing. Return of the lost memory. [Greding, l.c., p.650.]. He remembers long forgotten things. [Wiedemann, l.c.]. He remembered things that happened three years before. [Med. chir. Wahrnehmungen aus verschiednen Sprachen ubersetzt, Altenb., vii, p.69.]. Lively memory (curative effect) (aft. 24 h.). Violent headache. [Lambergen, -Greding, l.c., p.669.]. Headache as though the brain was numb. His whole head feels heavy, as from intoxication. A weight in the upper part of the forehead, which causes vertigo and as it were intoxication (aft. 14 d.). The head feels heavy as though he would fall asleep; he is disinclined for everything. Headache, only over the eyes, like a weight in the head, in the morning on waking, and when he touches the eyes it hurts. Feeling of weight with violent aching in the whole occiput (aft. 2 1/2 h.). Weight of the head as if it would fall down. In the morning headache as if something in the forehead above the eyebrows sunk down, which hinders the opening of the eyes (aft. 4 h.). An aching feeling of weight, from the centre of the brain towards the temples, with diminution of the hearing in both ears. Aching in the right side of the crown, later shifting into the left side, then back again into the right. [Mkl1.] [f:h1]. Aching headache, especially in the forehead (aft. 2 d.). (f-h1]. Constant dull aching headache in the side of the head (aft. 5, 24 h.). [f:-h1]. Painful aching feeling in the head, especially in the lower part of the forehead, just above the nose, intolerable on stepping. Headache above the orbits, as if the brain were pressed in, so that he must shut his eyes. Aching pain beneath the right frontal protuberance, which soon afterwards involves the whole forehead (aft. 10 m.). Violent aching under the right frontal protuberance. The aching pain under the frontal bone only occasionally declines to return again in still greater intensity. Aching pain beneath the frontal protuberances, in the morning soon after waking, on getting up. Violent outward-pressing pain in left frontal protuberance. (f-h1]. Violent inward-pressure in the left temple, which on leaning this side of the head on something extends over the whole of the anterior half of the brain (aft. 3/4 h.). Violent outward-pressure in the whole of the left half of the brain, especially severe in the forehead (aft. 2 1/4 h.). Aching pain in the right temporal region, which on leaning the head on the hand changes into a bursting pain, and extends to the right frontal protuberance (aft. 8 h.). Pressure in the head, now here, now there, that each time involves large spaces. [f£.h1]. Aching headache in the forehead, so bad on moving that it made the eyes close, alleviated when sitting; he must lie down, whereupon it went off; on standing up it immediately returned, for two days, not aggravated either by eating or by drinking; as soon as he goes into the open air he feels as if the forehead would be pressed in, just as if a heavy stone lay on it; the third day it went off completely when sitting in the room. An aching deep in the brain over the whole head, whilst and after walking in the open air. Headache pressing like a stone in the forehead, ameliorated by laying the head down and stooping forwards, with dilated pupils and whining ill- humour about trifles (aft. 3 h.). Tensive pressure in the right side of forehead. Tensive pressure in the left side of the crown and in the forehead (aft. 24 h.). (Hrr.]. Headache as if the head was screwed together from both sides and thereby became narrower. [f:h1]. A constant expansion of the whole brain. Violent pressing-outwards in the whole head, as though it would burst (aft. 3 h.). Headache as if the brain would be pressed out, just above the orbits in the forehead, which hinders the opening of the eyes, and compels lying down, with extreme contraction of the pupils and very low voice (aft. 5, 24 h.). On stooping forwards pain as if all would come out at the forehead. [Stf. ]. Sensation as if the brain pressed towards the forehead, which immediately went off when he bent the head backwards a little (aft. 1 1/4 h.). On coughing the feeling of pressing asunder in the head is much more severe (aft. 3 1/2 h.). In the open air the feeling of bursting in the head is very severe, and he is afraid to cough on account of increasing the pain (aft. 4h.). Throbbing pressing in the left side of the occiput (aft. 5 h.). [f:-h1]. On account of pain in the forehead he must often stand still when walking, at every step it feels as if the brain in the forehead sank and rose; this was alleviated by pressing strongly on it (aft. 6 days.). Strong pulsation of the blood-vessels in the forehead, and pain as if the bone were raised up. On awaking beating of the blood-vessels in the head and in most parts of the body. Violent throbbing in the brain from before backwards and to both sides; externally it ends in painful shoots. Aching gnawing headache on the right side of the upper part of the head down to the ear, produced by transient gnawing pain in the hollow tooth (aft. 9 h.). Aching shooting in the temples from within outwards (aft. 1/2 h.). Cutting pressure in the temples from within outwards, always becoming more violent, extending through the brain, and there turning into severe throbbing, continuing in all positions. Tearing pressure in the head, now here, now there, especially in the forehead and temporal region. Tearing pressure in the right temple and crown, that spreads out in various directions. Tearing pressure in the head here and there (aft. 5 h.). Drawing aching headache. A drawing in the head towards the forehead, as if the brain would expand. Drawing pain from the temple to above the right orbit. A drawing downwards at the temples and in the right orbit. (f-h1]. Boring and throbbing in the right side of the head, like that in the cheek, aggravated by every movement. Boring and aching headache during the day on various parts, in the evening shooting. Boring pain under the right frontal protuberance, soon after awaking in the morning. Incessant drawing and outstretching headache, as if something rocked or swayed about in it by jerk. Jerking headache, which became extremely violent on walking quickly or going quickly upstairs, and at every step jerked downwards like a weight in the occiput (aft. 48 h.). The whole head is affected with shooting pain, chiefly in the forehead. (Stf. ]. Obtuse shoots in the left temple from within outwards. In the whole forehead slight shooting headache (aft. 1 1/2 h.). [f:-h1]. Sharp shooting outwards in both frontal protuberances (aft. 2 h.). [f:-h1]. Excessively violent headache of obtuse or aching shoots, which dart through the brain from all sides. In the right temple violent shooting pain for a quarter of an hour (aft. 25h.). Some obtuse stitches in the left side of the occiput. In the right frontal protuberance severe shooting, aggravated by stooping forwards, lessened by touch (aft. 5 m.). Stabbing through the head as with a two-edged knife, in the evening. Stabs as with a knife from one temple to the other. In the evening some large stitches in the occiput close behind the ear, as rapid as lightning, so that he could have cried out (aft. 6 d.). In the right side of the head cutting stabs as with a two-edged knife which extend at one time into the sinciput, then into the crown, and then into the occiput, so that he cannot lie on either side. Three violent strong shoots through the head from the forehead to the occiput, whereupon all the previous headache suddenly disappears (aft. 3 1/4 h.). Shooting tearing in the head above the right orbit. Cutting tearing pain in the head that extends from one part to another. {Hrr.]. Burning tearing pain in the left frontal protuberance (aft. 4 h.). Tearing pain in the right side of the crown, aggravated by movement. ({Hrr.]. Tearing in the forehead externally. Tearing in the forehead. Tearing above the eyebrows. Violent pains in the head of a tearing kind, in the sinciput (aft. 8 h.). Headache on the crown, a twisting, sometimes also digging, sometimes tearing; the pain was much aggravated by external pressure; her skull appeared to her quite thin, as though it might be pressed through. Cold feeling in the brain, in the middle of the forehead. Drawing in the forehead. Drawing pain in the frontal bone and in the nape, when at rest and when moving. A headache apparently tearing asunder the sutures of the skull, as if a lever were inserted in order to burst open the head. Sensation in the brain as of splashing water. [Buchholz, in Hufel. Journal, v, I, p.252.]. On stooping forwards the blood rushes towards the forehead. [f:h1]. On stooping the blood mounts into the head and he becomes heavy and as if giddy. Ebullition of the blood to the head, without internal heat of head; when he leaned the head back it seemed to him that the blood rushed in. [f.-h1]. Heat in the head (aft. 1/4 h.). External pain in the whole head like that from rough pulling and rumpling the hair; pain remains in the scalp. Gnawing headache externally on the frontal protuberances. [f:-h1]. Fine shooting burning on the left frontal protuberance (aft. 1/4 h.). {Htn.]. A cutting headache on the left side near the occipital protuberance. On the right side of the head, and at the same time in the right arm, drawing pain, when at rest (after dinner). Very transient cramp pain on the right side of the vertex (aft. 11 h.). Cramp pain on the root of the nose. [f:-h1]. Severe cramp pain on the frontal protuberance, that extends downwards over the zygoma to the lower jaw. External feeling of contraction of the frontal and eye muscles. Scratching itching on the forehead (aft. 1 h.). A painful boil on the temple. Red painless pimples break out on the temple, on the right corner of the mouth, and on the chin; on scratching bloody water exudes (aft. 13 h.). {Lr.]. Swelling of the head. [Kr.- Munch, On Belladonna-Horst, Opera, ii, p.488.]. Great swelling of the head and redness all over the body. [ Munch, in Richter's Biblioth., v, p.387.]. Falling out of the hair, for an hour (aft. 24 h.). The hairs, which were previously idioelectric, are no longer so (aft. 24 h.). The external head is so sensitive that the slightest touch, even the pressure of the hair, causes her pain. Restless expression. [Boucher, in Jour. de Med., xxiv, 310.]. Distorted features. [Boucher, l.c.]. Paleness of face. [Sicelius, l.c.]. Pale face with thirst. [Greding, l.c., p.650.]. Pale face with increased appetite. [Greding, l.c., p.650.]. Sudden paleness of face for a considerable time. [Greding, l.c., p.677 (16).]. Often extreme pallor of face, instantaneously changing into redness of face, with cold cheeks and hot forehead. [Greding, l.c., p.662.]. Hot feeling in face without external redness (aft. 8 h.). Burning hot feeling in the whole face, without redness of cheeks and without thirst, with moderately warm body and cold feet (aft. 4 h.). Creeping hot feeling in the face beneath the skin (aft. 1/4 h.). [f:-h1]. Burning heat over the face, without thirst (aft. 10 h.). [fth1]. Unusual redness of the face. Great redness and heat in the face, without perspiration (aft. 24, 30 h.). (Mk1.]. Very red, hot face, with icy-cold limbs. Glowing redness of face, with violent indescribable pains in head. [Stf. ]. Heat and redness only on the head. Perspiration only on the face. Rush of blood to the head, red cheeks. [Buchave, l.c.]. Great heat and redness of the cheeks. [Buchave, l.c.] [f:-h1]. Face very swollen and hot. [Buchave, l.c.]. Redness and heat in the whole face, as if he had drunk much wine. {Hbg.]. Heat in the face all day, as if the blood had mounted to the head from drinking wine (aft. 12 h.). Blood-red face. [Sauter, l.c. (1).]. Swollen skin of face, as if an eruption were going to break out. {Sauter, l.c. (ID).]. Face bluish red, with great heat of the body, in the evening. [Wiedemann, l.c.]. Scarlet redness of the face and chest during sleep. [Schaffer, in Hufel. Journ., vi.]. Scarlet redness of the skin of the body, especially of the face, with marked activity of the brain. [Wetzler, in Annalen der Heilkunde, 1811, Febr.]. Dark red spots on the face, resembling the rash of scarlet fever, with full pulse. [Wiedemann, l.c.]. Along with sudden rigor great dulness of head and sight, red eyes, and swollen face covered with very small, unequal, dark red spots, especially on the forehead. [Greding, l.c., p.685 (19).]. In the morning on awaking a small bluish-red spot on the left cheek, which gradually enlarges until the bluish-red swelling involves the whole cheek, with burning and shooting in the actual red part, and boring and throbbing in the whole cheek, much aggravated by movement; after some days the other cheek swelled, and the swelling lasted eight days. (f-h1]. Red swollen face. [May, l.c.]. Red swollen face with staring eyes. [Justi, in Hufel. Jour., vii, 4, p.65.]. Swollen face. The face was red and swollen, but the rest of the body pale. [Grimm, Lc.]. Swelling of the cheeks, with burning pain. Hard large swelling on the face about the nose and eye, with swelling of the parotid gland on the opposite side, lasting five days. [Greding, l.c., p.668.]. Swelling of the left cheek about the nose and eye, which comes on in the afternoon, increases the following day with heat, and lasts five days. [Greding, l.c., p.667.]. Swollen face. [ Munch, l.c.]. Swelling of the face, and especially of the lips. [Lambergen, l.c.]. An uninterrupted quivering (and winking) of both eyelids. All day long an uninterrupted trembling and quivering of the right upper eyelid, that at last becomes painful. Expanded eyelids, eyes standing wide open. Throbbing pain in the lower eyelid towards the inner canthus, with great inflammatory swelling at that point, with much lachrymation, for half an hour (aft. 32 h.). [Mk1.]. The eyes close and become watery. Heaviness in the eyes, especially the upper lid. After waking in the morning, the eyes again close involuntarily; she cannot keep them open until she gets up. Itching stitches in the inner canthi, which only leave off for a short time on rubbing (aft. 1 h.). The inner canthus of the left eye is very painful, even when lightly touched. Smarting in both eyes. Involuntary lachrymation of the eyes. Salt water runs constantly out of the eyes. Lachrymation of the eyes. Dryness of the eyes (of the nose, mouth, pharynx). [Wasserberg, in Stoll, Ratio Medendi, iii, p.403.]. Burning dry feeling in both eyes, alternately worse in one or the other (aft. 7 h.). (Mk1.]. Pain and burning in the eyes. [Greding, l.c., p.644.]. Increased heat and hot feeling in the eyes. Feeling of heat in the eyes; it is as if they were enveloped in a hot vapour. Photophobia; he avoids looking into the light. [Justi, l.c.]. Burning of the eyes, accompanied by painful itching; but both cease when the eyes are pressed upwards (aft. 28 h.). [Mk1.]. In the morning the white of the eye is red-streaked, with aching pain. [f:-h1]. Inflammation of the eyes; injection of the veins of the white of the eye, with a tickling sensation. Inflammation of the eyes; the conjunctiva is traversed by red blood- vessels, with shooting pain; the eyes water. Shooting in the eyes towards the interior. Yellowness of the white of the eye. In the morning the eyes are quite sealed up with matter. [Mkl., Kr.] [f:-h1]. Swelling and purulent inflammation of the left punctum lachrymale, at first with burning pain, afterwards with aching pain, for three days (aft. 4 d.). [Mk1.]. A general aching in both eyes, as if hard spring water had got into the eyes. When she closes the eyes, an aching pain deep in the eyeball. A cloudy aching comes in the right orbit and goes from that alternately into the forehead and back again. Aching and watering of the eyes, especially in the morning. [f:-h1]. Creeping aching pain in the eyes, as if they were full of sand; she must rub them (aft. Lh). Aching in the eyes, as if sand had got into them (aft. 2 1/2 h.). Aching in the eyes, as from a grain of sand. [Greding, loc. cit., p.650.- Mk1.]. Pain in the orbits; sometimes it is as if the eyes were torn out, sometimes (and this more persistently) as though they were pressed into the head, to which a pain is superadded, that presses from the forehead upon the eyes. A tearing in the eyes proceeding from the inner canthi. (f-h1]. Drawing pain under the left eye upwards. Contracted pupils, difficult to dilate. [f:h1]. Very contracted pupils all day; they only dilate in the evening. [f:-h1]. Contracted pupils (aft. 10 m.). Contracted pupils (aft. 1 1/4 h.). Contracted pupils (aft. 2 1/2 h.). The dilatation of pupils commenced after half an hour, and then increased gradually. Dilated pupils after 3 1/2 hours. [Sauter, l.c. (D.-Ln.]. The pupils are very dilated in the evening, even when the light is held close to the eye (aft. 12 h.). Dilated pupils (aft. 14, 15 h.). The pupils are more dilated, from the third day onwards. Dilated immovable pupils. [May, l.c.]. Extremely dilated pupils. [Boucher, l.c.]. A small white pustule in the left, extremely dilated pupil. (f.h1]. Extremely dilated pupils (from laying a fresh belladonna leaf on an ulcer beneath the eye). [Ray, Histor. plant., lib. 13, cap.23.] [f:-h1]. Sometimes complete loss of, sometimes merely diminished, vision, with enormously dilated and quite immovable pupils. [Elfes, in Rust's Magaz., vol. xxi, pt.3.]. Complete dilatation of the pupil of the right eye and blindness for three weeks (from the juice of the plant injected into the eye). [Daries, Diss. de Belladonna, Lips., 1776, pp.34, 35.]. Obscuration of vision from dilated pupils. [Buchave, l.c.]. Obscuration of vision, with extremely dilated pupils. [Greding, L.c., vol. ii, p.324.]. Blindness, the pupil of the right eye extremely dilated and incapable of contracting. [Greding, l.c., p.662.]. Great dimness of vision. [Justi, l.c.]. Before the eyes, as if dim, dark, and black (aft. 1 1/4h.). Blindness. [Hasenest, Acta Nat. Cur., vol. iii, obs.35.]. Amaurosis for three days; he cannot read print. [Hasenest, l.c.]. He awakes blind. [El. Camerarius, in his Obs. and in Wepfer, Hist. Cic.]. The eyes are blind and stand open. [El. Camerarius, I.c.]. Extreme weakness of sight. [Ollenroth, l.c.]. Transient blindness, with headache. [Greding, l.c., p.679. (17).] (f-h1]. Dimness of vision, alternating with convulsions in hands and feet, dulness of head and weariness of limbs. [Greding, l.c., p.683 (18).]. Dimness of vision, dryness of mouth, and pain in belly. [Greding, l.c., p.606.]. Dulness of sight for three hours. [Greding, l.c., p.679 (17).] (f.h1]. Along with dulness of sight trembling in all the limbs. [Greding, L.c., p.643.]. Long-sightedness (presbyopia), as in old age. [Lottinger, l.c.]. Feeling as if he could see nothing, and yet he saw when he tried to see anything, and strained the eyes to do so. The letters tremble and quiver, of a golden and blue colour, when reading. She sees on the ceiling of the room a white star as large as a plate, and light silver clouds pass over it from left to right-several times and in various places. Large bright sparks from the eyes. He sees sparks before the eyes. [Ziegler, l.c.]. On moving the eyelids he sees sparks, as from electricity. [Ziegler, Lc.]. Sees objects double. [Henning, -Sicelius, l.c.-Stf. ]. He sees nothing at all near, and everything double at a distance. [f:-h1]. He sees objects multiplied and dark. [Sauter, l.c. (ID.]. He sees objects inverted. [Henning, l.c.]. Feeling in the eyes as if they stood farther out. Projecting eyes, with dilated pupils (aft. 6 h.). [Mkl1.]. Staring eyes. [Mueller, in Horn's Archiv, ix.]. Bold look. [Dumoulin, in Jour. de M,d., xi, Ao-t.]. The eyes are projecting and sparkling. [Grimm, l.c.]. Glittering (glassy) eyes. [Ziegler, l.c.]. The eyes are very animated, with fully dilated pupils (aft. 20 h.). {Boucher, |.c.]. The eyes are red, glittering (glassy), and roll about in the head. [Sauter, Le. (ID).]. Squeezing pressure on the left zygoma. A tearing and drawing under the right zygoma (aft. 1/4 h.). [f.-h1]. Pressure under the right zygoma. When chewing, in the right maxillary joint a violent shooting, extending into the ear, which continues after chewing, but more as a twitching (aft. 1 h.). Fine stitches in the cavity of the maxillary joint (aft. 1 h.). Stitches from the upper j aw into the inner ear. [f:h1]. Stitches in the parotid gland. Violent stitch in the right parotid gland, extending to the auricle, when it terminates in a crampy pain (aft. 2 h.); next day the same about the same hour (aft. 26 h.). Tearing pain on the posterior side of the cartilage of the left ear. Tearing pressure on the lower half of the cartilage of the right ear. ({Hrr.]. Tearing in the right auricle, which extended backwards. Tearing downwards in the inner and outer ear. Tearing pain in the right auricle, and downwards in the whole side of the face (aft. 24 h.). Stitches in the external meatus auditorius. Pinching in the ears, first in the right, then in the left, immediately after the hiccup. A disagreeable aching in the meatus auditorius, as if a finger were bored in. Feeling in the external meatus auditorius, as if some one pressed upon it. [fth1]. A very disagreeable feeling in the right ear, as if it were forcibly torn out of the head. Alternately out-tearing and in-pressing pain in the ears and temples, alternating with a similar pain in the orbits. Earache in the left ear (aft. 5 d.). Sharp blows in the inner ear, with squeezing, like earache. Near the right ear boring pain. Aching tearing behind the right ear (aft. 1/2 h.). Behind the left ear to the neck the muscles are painful, as if they were strongly pressed, the same in the frontal muscles. A flying stitch darts from the ear into the chin (aft. 1 h.). Stitches in the inner ear, with impaired hearing in it. Stitches in the inner ear during eructation from the stomach with the taste of food (aft. 12 h.). Drawing pain from the ears to the nape. Violent pressure on the mastoid processes below the ear. Cutting blows through the mastoid process inwards (aft. 12 h.). Purulent discharge from the ears for twenty days. Increased sensitiveness of the auditory organ. [Sauter, l.c. (II).] (f-h1]. First a noise like trumpets and drums in the ears and like roaring (immediately); afterwards humming and buzzing, worst when sitting, better when standing and lying, still better when walking. Noises in the ears. [Vicat, l.c., p.181.]. Rushing noise in the ears, vertigo, and dull bellyache. [Greding, |.c., p.658.]. Wind rushes out of the ears. [Greding, l.c., p.658.]. In the moming, immediately after waking, a fluttering and bubbling before the ears. Deafness, as if a skin were stretched before the ears. Difficulty of hearing. [Greding, l.c., p.604 (23).]. On the root of the nose a couple of small red lumps, paining like a fester, but only when touched (aft. 16 d.). Pimples break out on the cheeks and nose, rapidly fill with pus, and become covered with a scab. Very cold nose. [Greding, L.c., p.664.]. Smell before the nose like rotten eggs for a quarter of an hour (aft. 4 h.). Aching pain in the nasal bones. In the nose above the alae pain as if bruised on touching it externally. Too sensitive sense of smell; the odour of tobacco smoke and soot is intolerable to him (aft. 1 h.). Nose-bleeding (immediately). [f:-h1]. Nose-bleeding at night. Nose-bleeding in the morning. [f:-h1]. Painful drawing over the left half of the nose. Creeping in the point of the nose, that goes off on rubbing. Fine stitches in the point of the nose from the evening onwards through the night. Sudden redness of the point of the nose, with burning sensation. A very painful left nostril, that is plugged up with matter in the morning (aft. 6 weeks). Under the nose fine stitches (aft. 1/2 h.). Great swelling of the upper lip; it is stiff on opening the mouth. (f-h1]. Painful ulcerous state of the nostrils at the side where they unite with the upper lip. The nostrils and the angles of the lips are ulcerated, but neither itch nor are painful. Drawing in the upper lip followed by red swelling. Abscess of the upper lip, causing painful swelling, with fever, headache, and loss of appetite, ending in free discharge of pus. [Lambergen, Lc.]. A white-headed pimple under the left ala nasi, without pain. Ulcerated angle of the mouth, exactly where the two lips unite, with uncommon tearing pains round about, even when at rest and per se (aft. 5 h.). [£.h1]. Sore feeling in the corners of the mouth, as if they would become ulcerated (aft. 5, 6, 7 days). Small pimples, one on the upper lip near the right ala nasi, covered with a scab, another under the border of the lower lip and on the inner skin of the lower lip, all with smarting pain as from salt water. Small pale-red pimples at the corners of the mouth without sensation; they soon go off without suppurating. On the upper lip a pimple, with creeping sensation when let alone, but with itching shooting when touched. In the corner of the lips an ulcer with red border and eroding itching. [f:-h1]. On the lower external lip-edge burning pain and small vesicles (aft. 24 h.). The lips, especially the upper lip, crack in the middle on sneezing and coughing. A pimple on the border of the lip, equidistant from the middle and the corner, which becomes transformed into an ulcer covered by a scab, and pains like an inflamed part. Spasmodic movements of the lips. [Mueller, |.c.]. The right corner of the mouth is drawn outwards. [Greding, l.c., p.662.]. A spasm draws the mouth awry (risus sardonicus). [Weinmann, in Gmelin's Pflanzengifte, p.296.]. Mouth drawn awry by spasms. [De St. Martin, l.c.]. Bloody foam before the mouth (shortly before death). [Commerc. lit. Nor., 1731.]. Bloody foam before the mouth, shaking of the head, and grinding of the teeth from early morning till noon. [Greding, l.c., p.691 (22).]. Pimples betwixt lips and chin, filled with pus, with burning smarting pain, especially painful at night (aft. 6 days). A pimple with smarting eroding pain externally, below and to the side of the lip. A pimple on the side of the chin, with itching shooting, but more shooting than itching; this sensation is removed by scratching. Several small pimples on the chin. A number of small miliary papules on the chin, with burning pain on touching them (aft. 3 d.). Sharp stitches on the chin (immediately). A nestling spasm-like feeling in the chin. Trismus; impossibility of opening the jaws on account of painful stiffness of the chewing muscles (by day). [fth1]. Closure of the jaws, trismus. [Hasenest, -May, l.c.]. She clinched her teeth together so that they could not be separated, though great force was employed, with twitchings in all the limbs and chilliness. [ Munch, in Richter's Bibliothek, v, p.566.]. She feels as if the lower jaw were drawn backwards; pushing it forwards causes great pain, biting causes horrible pains. At the angle of the lower jaw a red boil, which is hard and not painful unless pressed on, which causes shooting pain. On the lower border of the right lower jaw sharp stitches. (f-h1]. Throbbing on the lower border of the lower jaw (aft. 1/2 h.). In the lower jaw (in the glands?) (a jerking drawing?) pain that darted in rapidly and quickly went off. Swollen cervical glands, which are painful at night; on swallowing they do not hurt. Stitches in a gland at a side of the neck. On the left side of the neck, in the cervical muscles, a cramplike tensive sensation, even when not moving (aft. 1/4 h.). Her head is drawn backwards; it buries itself at night deep in the pillow. Stiffness of the neck, so that he cannot lay the head sideways. Stiffness of the nape. Drawing in the cervical muscles. In the right cervical muscles drawing aching pain. Fine stitches in the pit of the throat. Aching sensation on the left side of the larynx, which is increased by external pressure (aft. 1/2 h.). Feels the beating of the cervical arteries. Aching pain in the nape close up to the occiput, which is not altered by movement (aft. 3 h.). Violent stitches in the nape often renewed, in the region of the second and third cervical vertebrae, on holding up the head (aft. 3/4 h.). [f:-h1]. Violent grinding of the teeth. [ Munch, l.c.]. Grinding of the teeth with much foam before the mouth of the smell of rotten eggs. [Greding, l.c., p.692 (22).]. Grinding of the teeth and convulsion of the right arm. [Greding, l.c., p.687 (20).]. Grinding of the teeth with copious flow of saliva from the mouth. [Greding, l.c., p.653.]. Extremely painful swelling of the gums on the right side, with fever and chilly feeling. Heat in the gums; itching and throbbing in them. Very tiresome itching on the gums, with pains in the throat. [Baldinger, l.c.]. The gum at a hollow tooth bleeds (aft. 6 d.). On sucking with the tongue at the hollow teeth, blood flows out of them, without pain. A drawing in the upper front molars of the right side, remaining the same under all conditions. Toothache with drawing in the ear. He wakes up after midnight with violent tearing (?) in the teeth. By the contact of the open air a uniform simple toothache, like the pain of excoriation (aft. 1/4 h.). Not while eating, but only several minutes after eating the toothache comes on, increases gradually to a high degree, and declines equally gradually; it does not come on after drinking. Toothache in the evening after lying down and during intellectual work; a dull pain in the nerve of the roots of the teeth, almost like the pain of excoriation, and when worse like a continual cutting. Toothache; a sharp drawing from the ear downwards into the hollow teeth of the upper jaw, where the pain became boring, slighter whilst eating, more severe after eating, never quite leaving off by day, but worst at night and completely hindering sleep (after drinking coffee it changed to a dull jerking and boring). Dull drawing in right upper row of teeth all night, the pain prevented sleep; the painful part was somewhat swollen (with burning pain) and hot to the touch; sometimes painful jerks in the teeth. A fine shooting pain in an upper hollow molar all day, allowing him but little sleep at night, followed by swelling of cheek. (A (transient) digging toothache.). (The incisors feel too long.). Teeth painful on biting as if the roots were festering and would break off. Single, very painful twitches or throbs in the root-nerves of one or several teeth. In the mouth a feeling of increased space, just as if the tongue was further down than usual. Feeling as if the tongue were asleep, dead and numb, in the morning. Feeling of coldness and dryness in the fore half of the tongue. [f:h1]. The whole tongue is painful, especially when touched. (f-h1]. Cracked, white furred tongue, with great secretion of saliva. On the middle of the white furred tongue severe smarting pain, as from a vesicle (aft. 3 d.). [£h1]. On the tip of the tongue a feeling as if a vesicle were there which has burning pain on being touched, for two days. The lingual papillae are bright red, inflamed, and much swollen (aft. 3 d.). Speaking is very difficult for him; his voice is piping. Slimy mouth, with the feeling as if he had a bad smell from the mouth, as when the stomach is deranged. In the morning the mouth is full of mucus; he must wash it out from time to time; after eating the mucus disappears. Slimy mouth in the morning on awaking, with aching headache (both lasting but a short time.). In the morning on awaking he smelt very badly out of the mouth. [f:-h1]. Great feeling of dryness in the mouth, with very irritable humour; yet the mouth and tongue look moist. Great feeling of dryness in the mouth; there was very little viscid mucus on the tongue, and the lips were hot and their skin peeled off. Viscid mucus in the mouth and dry feeling. Dryness in the mouth. [Ziegler, l.c.]. Great feeling of dryness in the moist mouth, with stickiness and great thirst. Great dryness in the throat. [Cullen, Mat. Med., ii, p.307.]. Dryness in the mouth with thirst. Aridity of the mouth, as if the inner skin had been removed by something acrid. [Lottinger, l.c.]. Dryness of the mouth that can scarcely be got rid of. Feeling of excessive dryness in the mouth, and yet the tongue was always moist. [Stf. ]. Excessive dryness of the mouth that caused constriction in the throat. (Stf. ]. Dryness in the mouth, the fauces, and the nose. [Buchave-Lambergen, Lc.]. He cannot swallow on account of dryness of mouth, fauces, and nose. [Buchave, l.c.]. Gmelin and Faber.). Flow of blood from mouth and nose. [Wagner, L.c. (II).]. Scraping scratching on the palate, occurring by itself. Fine tearing on the inner surface of the angle of the left side of the interior maxilla, on the left tonsil and behind it, unaltered by touching; violent tearing on swallowing (aft. 2 d.). Dryness in the fauces and burning on the tongue. [Ollenroth, |.c.]. Burning sensation in the fauces. [Henning, l.c.]. Transient but frequently recurring contraction of the esophagus, more on swallowing than at other times, each time followed by a scratching pain in the region of the epiglottis as if there was something raw and sore there. {Ln.]. Sore throat; when swallowing scraping in the palate and as if rubbed raw there. Throat affection; narrowing (contraction) of the gullet, whereby swallowing is impeded (aft. 3 h.). On swallowing, a feeling in the throat as if all were too narrow there, as if contracted, as though nothing could get rightly down (aft. 2 h.). [f:h1]. She could not swallow solid food. At the commencement of the meal proper taste of the food, but all at once everything tasted partly too salt, partly like nothing and insipid, with feeling in the throat (pit of the throat) as if the ingesta would be thrown up again. Bread smells and tastes sour to him. [fth1]. Bread tastes sour to him. The bread tastes sour to her. Disgust at milk, which at other times she generally drank and with relish; it has a disgusting very repulsive smell and (sourish-bitter) taste to her, which, however, goes off on continuing to drink. In the evening the bread and butter, at least the last portion of it, tastes very sour, whereupon generally some heartburn ensued, which lasted two hours (for eight successive evenings) (aft. 4 d.). Anorexia, with empty feeling and hunger; on commencing to eat he relishes the food and eats as usual. Increased appetite (curative action). Appetite for water-soup and bread and butter, but for nothing else. After eating but little, a peculiar contracting feeling in the stomach. (Mk1.]. After eating cough and great thirst. Astonishing thirst in the evening with watery taste, but all drinks disgusted her. Strong thirst for cold drinks, without heat (aft. 7 h.). Nausea, inclination to vomit, and such great thirst that she must drink enormous quantities of water. [Baldinger, l.c.]. Vomiting in the evening. [Greding, l.c., p.650.]. Ineffectual effort to vomit. He awakes thrice about midnight; he heaves thrice as if to vomit, but without result, with the sweat of anxiety. Ineffectual effort to vomit, empty retching. Several times violent hiccup. Violent hiccup that jerked her upwards, whereupon she became deaf until the next attack. Violent hiccup about midnight. [Greding, l.c., p.653.]. Something intermediate between eructation and hiccup. Hiccupy eructation; a spasm compounded of eructation and hiccup. At night eructation, with profuse sweat. [Greding, l.c., p.669 (14).]. After hiccup convulsions of the head and limbs, then nausea and weariness. [Greding, l.c., p.672 (14).]. Very profuse, long-continued sweat, which stains the linen dark. [Greding, l.c., p.667.]. Sudden breaking out of general sweat, which disappears as suddenly. Sweat as soon as he covers himself with the bed-clothes, especially on the upper parts. It is only the parts that are covered with the bed-clothes that perspire, in the evening. Quite early in the morning (about 2 or 3 a.m.) after awaking, perspiration when the arms are covered, which goes off when he uncovers them. In the morning, intermitting sweat, that rose from the feet up into the face, where it was particularly profuse; but immediately afterwards she got cool again. During the febrile heat there occurs general perspiration when the hands are covered by the bedclothes, but when she uncovers them general chilliness. Trembling. [Horst.-De Launay d'Hermont.-Eb. Gmelin, |.c.] (f-h1]. He readily starts, especially when any one approaches him. [f:-h1]. By day, great anxiety; she cannot rest anywhere; she feels as if she should run away. Great anxiety about the praecordia. [Wagner, l.c. (I).]. Very anxious and fearful. Anxiety in the cardiac region (aft. 3 h.). Anxiety, during the menses. [Schmucker, |.c.-Lambergen, l.c.]. Frequent groaning (grunting) especially in the morning, without being able to tell why, or what pain makes him do so. Grunting and groaning at each expiration. [f:h1]. Grunting and groaning in sleep. Sighs. [Eb. Gmelin, l.c.]. Groaning alternating with hopping and dancing. [Mardorf, l.c.] (f-h1]. With a sudden cry he trembles in hands and feet. [Greding, l.c., p.644.]. Much anxiety, and an hour afterwards perspiration. [Henning, 1.c.]. Events which hitherto he had hoped for with pleasure, now appear to him in an anxious light; they appear to him frightful and horrible. [f.-h1]. In the momentary intervals, when free from fury, complaints of intolerable anxiety, so that she wished to die. [Baldinger, l.c.]. About noon and evening, precordial anxiety, headache, redness of face and bitterness of mouth. [Greding, 1.c., p.671 (14).]. Anxiety and restlessness. [Eb. Gmelin, |.c.]. Restlessness. [Boucher, l.c.]. Great restlessness, she cannot remain long seated in one place; she is driven all about. Continual turning about of the whole body, as in chorea. [Boucher, Le.]. Incessant movement of the body, especially of the arms, with unaltered pulse. [Boucher, l.c.]. Great movement hither and thither in bed. [Boucher, l.c.]. Unconnected talking in the evening. [Ackermann, l.c.]. Raving, delirium. [Ziegler.-May.-El. Camerarius.-Med. Chirurg. Wahrnehm., vii. Eb. Gmelin.-Buc‘hoz, l.c.]. Continual delirium. [Horst, l.c.]. After eating the delirium is allayed. He makes preparations to go home. [Greding, l.c., p.688 (21).]. He is delirious, and cries out in his dreams that he must go home, because everything is on fire there. [Greding, l.c., p.688 (21).]. Raves about wolves being in the room; at the same time full pulse. [G-Ch, l.c.]. Delirious talk about dogs surrounding him. [Hufeland, Jour., xvi.]. He is beside himself, raves, talks much about dogs, his arm and face swell. [ Munch, L.c.]. At night he talks nonsense, by day he is sensible. [Greding, l.c., p.676 (16).]. Nocturnal delirium, which is allayed by day. [Greding, l.c., p.655.] [f:-h1]. At one time he is delirious, at another he answers sensibly and bemoans himself. [El. Camerarius, l.c.]. Delirium in recurring paroxysms. [Albrecht, l.c.]. He murmurs as in sleep. [Hasenest, l.c.]. She talks nonsense and extreme folly. [Grimm, l.c.]. Foolish chatter. [Boucher.-El. Camerarius (aft. 6 h.).Buchave, l.c.- Greding, l.c., p.650.]. She chatters nonsense with great rapidity. [Sauter, l.c. (1).]. Chattering like a mad person, with staring, protruding eyes. [Buchave, l.c.]. Chattering, lascivious. [Greding, l.c., p.663.]. After the chattering fit speechlessness. [Buchave, l.c.]. Gay mania. [Sauvages, l.c.]. Sits unoccupied behind the stove; she tries to compose songs, and sings aloud songs of a gay, though nonsensical character; sometimes she whistled, but would neither eat nor drink; at the same time she neither heard nor saw anything, with paleness of face and sweat on the forehead. He sings and trills. [Med. Chir. Wahrnehm., vii.]. Excessively gay disposition, he is disposed to sing and whistle (evening) (aft. 13 h.). Involuntary, almost loud laughter, without having any laughable thoughts. He smiles for a long time to himself. [Greding, l.c., p.650.]. Frequent laughter. [Greding, l.c., p.651.]. Laughing and singing she touches surrounding objects all day. [Greding, l.c., p.690 (22).]. She bursts out in loud laughter, sings, and touches things near her. [Greding, l.c., p.679 (17).]. Loud laughter. [Grimm.-Dumoulin.-Hochstetter, l.c. Med. Chirurg. Wahrnehm., vii.]. Uncontrollable loud laughter. [Carl, 1.c.]. Unrestrainedly and exuberantly merry, inclined to scold without cause, and to insult in a laughing humour. Excessive gaiety after supper, the vital powers extraordinarily increased for a quarter of an hour, followed by drowsiness. (f-h1]. Ridiculous grimaces; she takes hold of those about her, at one time she sits, at another she acts as if she were washing, at another as if counting money, at another as if she were drinking. [Hasenest, l.c.]. At one time he displays laughing madness, at another he talks sensibly (aft. 1, 16 h.). He does foolish, ridiculous tricks (aft. 1/2, 6, 8 h.). Various gesticulations. [Hochstetter, l.c.]. Insanity. [Hochstetter, 1.c.]. Insanity; they strip themselves, run about the streets in their shirts, gesticulate, dance, laugh loudly, chatter nonsense, and do foolish things. [Dillenius, 1.c.]. He walks lifting his feet high, as if he must step over things in his path, like a drunken person. [Sicelius, l.c.]. Violent shaking of the head. [Greding, 1.c., p.653.]. Great shaking of the head, foam before the mouth, and lost consciousness. [Greding, I.c., p.673 (14).]. She claps her hands above her head, with a short, very violent cough that threatens to suffocate her, at night. [Greding, l.c., p.691 (22).] (f-h1]. He claps his hands, wags his head from side to side, and viscid saliva hangs down in long strings from his lips. [Greding, l.c., p.691 (22).] (f-h1]. She distorts her facial muscles in a horrible way, protrudes her tongue to its full extent, clacks with her tongue, and retches as though she would vomit, in fits. [Greding, l.c.]. At one time he grasps hastily at those near him, at another he shrinks back in affright. [Sauter, l.c. (11).]. Weeping. [Dumoulin, l.c.]. Very excited humour, she is always disposed to weep. While walking, in the open air she is overcome by lachrymose anxiety; she is weary of life, and wishes to go into the water to drown herself. Lachrymose fearfulness (aft. 1/8 h., aft. 2, and within 8 h.). At first piteous weeping, which then passes into impatient and impassioned howling (with chilliness) (aft. 1 h.). Violent weeping, whining, and howling without cause, combined with fearfulness (aft. 2 to 8, aft. 8 to 12, rarely aft. 12 to 20 h.). Weeping and excessive crossness on awaking from sleep. Depression, unhappiness. [Boucher, l.c.]. He gets up at night and walks up and down buried in thought. [Greding, l.c., p.682 (18).]. Indisposed, indifferent to everything, defective activity of body and mind. [Mk1.] [f:-h1]. Hourly alternation of weeping and cross humour. Excessive indifference, for hours; one might take her life, she would not stir. Apathy; nothing can make an impression on her; after some days very sensitive cross humour; she has no pleasure in anything. Cheerless, peevish, disinclined for everything. Whining peevishness about trifles, with headache like pressure from a stone. Disinclined to speak. He wishes for solitude and quiet; all noise and visits from others are repugnant to him. Silent moroseness (aft. 8 h.), two days afterwards ordinary humour, but the following day again morose. Very morose and serious. He was cross about one thing and another. Very irritable humour with great dryness in the mouth. Great irritability and acuteness of the senses; everything tastes and smells stronger; the sense of touch, the sight, and the hearing are more acute, and the humour is more mobile and the thoughts more active (aft. 3 h.). Peevishness, nothing was right for him; he was angry with himself. Very excited; she soon loses her temper and then begins to weep. He is easily excited to anger, even by trifles. They stammered out violent language. [Dumoulin, l.c.]. Delirium either in recurring paroxysms or continued; it was at first merry, but afterwards changes into fury. [Vicat, l.c.]. Howling and crying out about trifles, which becomes worse when spoken to kindly, with pupils that easily dilate and very readily contract. [f:-h1]. Violent inclination to quarrel, which cannot be soothed. Delirium with wildness. [Hoyer, l.c.]. Fury. [Valentini.-Wierus.-Schreck, l.c.]. Fury; the boy does not know his parents. [Solenander, in Abhand. der Konigl. Acad. d. Wissench., Breslau, 1750, p.364.]. She tosses wildly about in bed (aft. 10 h.). [Sauter, Lc. (I).]. She tears her night-dress and bedclothes. [Sauter, I.c. (D.] (f-h1]. He strikes his face with his fists. [Greding, l.c., p.664.]. Maniacal fury with violence. Fury, with gnashing of the teeth and convulsions. [May, l.c.] (f-h1]. In place of eating what he desired he bit the wooden spoon to pieces, gnawed the dishes, and growled and barked like a dog. [ Munch, in Richter's Biblioth., v, p.564.]. Madness, in which the patient was often very merry, sang and cried out, then again spat and bit. [Elfes, l.c.]. She does nonsensical things, tears her clothes to rags, picks up stones from the ground and pelts those around her with them (aft. 2 h.). [Sauter, .c. (1).]. Fury; he injures himself and others and strikes about him. [Greding, Le., p.664.]. He tries to bite those about him at night. [Greding, l.c., p.682 (18).] [f.-h1]. Fury; she clutches those about her by the hair. [Mardorf, l.c.]. Along with burning heat of the body, with open, staring, and fixed eyes, such fury that she must constantly be held fast in order to prevent her attacking others, and when thus held so that she could not move she spat constantly at those about her. [Baldinger, l.c.]. After sleep excessive moroseness; he bites those about him. [Buchave, I.c.]. He bites at everything near him. [ Munch, 1.c.]. Inclination to bite those about him. [Dumoulin, l.c.]. Inclination to tear everything about her to pieces. [Dumoulin, |.c.]. He tears to pieces everything about him, bites, and spits. [Sauter, l.c. (ID).]. Throws off the bedclothes in his madness. [Eb. Gmelin, |.c.]. Tries to jump out of bed. [Eb. Gmelin, l.c.]. So anxious and distracted that she apprehends the approach of death. [Timmermann, Diss. Periculum Belladonnae.]. He fears death is nigh. [Eb. Gmelin, l.c.]. Fearful mistrust. [f:h1]. Fearful madness; he is afraid of an imaginary black dog, of the gallows, c. (more frequently in the first 12 hours, more rarely in the following hours). Madness; he thinks that his living body is putrefying. He tries to run away. [Sauter, l.c. (ID.]. He makes an excuse and runs out into the open fields. [ Munch, I.c.]. She tries to strangle herself, and begs those about her to kill her, for this time she must die. [Greding, l.c., p.690 (22).]. She begs those around her to kill her. [Greding, l.c., p.692 (22).].
